message:
test(fcp-conformance): edge cases for AuthClaims canonical CBOR

Three edge-case tests plus a fix for minimal_claims_golden_vector,
which was asserting numeric-ascending key order (the pre-6df66c79
invariant) and failing after that commit switched encoding to RFC
8949 bytewise lex order.

  * default_claims_encode_single_schema_version_entry — locks the
    minimum wire shape: AuthClaims::default() emits a 1-entry map
    carrying only SCHEMA_VERSION=0, with a lossless round-trip.
  * unknown_labels_silently_ignored_on_decode — forward-compat
    contract for future schema additions. Inserts an unknown
    positive (9999) and far-negative (-70000) label, re-canonicalizes
    through fcp_cbor::to_canonical_cbor, then confirms the decoder
    accepts the bytes and silently drops the extras.
  * schema_version_u16_boundary_round_trip — covers schema_version=0
    and u16::MAX, plus a crafted overflow (u16::MAX+1) that must
    surface OutOfRange rather than silently truncate.

minimal_claims_golden_vector now spells out the byte-lex ordering in
a docstring and asserts the expected key sequence directly (no more
local-sort comparison that masked the rule).

Verified with
  CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/fcp-review-A cargo test \
    -p fcp-conformance --test auth_claims_conformance
→ 8 passed, 0 failed.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
